metadata.seed: I have this certification program I took about two years ago that allowed me to be marketable out there and it's about to expire. I'm currently under pressure to retake the certification and I'm not quite ready. I will loose my license if I don't , time is running out and I currently feel lost in the predicament I have found myself.

patient.query: I have this certification program I took about two years ago that allowed me to be marketable out there and it's about to expire. I'm currently under pressure to retake the certification and I'm not quite ready. I will loose my license if I don't , time is running out and I currently feel lost in the predicament I have found myself.

automatic_thoughts: I will lose my license
I'm not quite ready
I feel lost in this predicament

patient.query: Yeah, I guess the pressure is really overwhelming right now. I have this constant worry that I’m not going to pass, and if I don’t, what does that say about me? In the past, I've always felt this knot in my stomach before exams or big projects. It really just makes me freeze, like I can’t think straight.

automatic_thoughts: I'm not going to pass
What does that say about me?

patient.query: I don’t know, it’s like this voice in my head telling me that I'm not smart enough, or that if I fail, everyone will see me as a failure. Sometimes I feel like I’m just pretending to be capable. It’s exhausting, and then I just end up avoiding studying altogether because it feels so impossible.

automatic_thoughts: I'm not smart enough
If I fail, everyone will see me as a failure
I'm just pretending to be capable

patient.query: I mean, I don’t have any proof, it’s just how I feel. I remember a time in high school when I didn’t do well on a test, and some of my friends joked about it. Ever since then, I guess I've been scared that if I slip up again, people will judge me. It feels like I’m always on display or something. It’s hard to shake that feeling.

automatic_thoughts: I don’t have any proof
if I slip up again, people will judge me
I’m always on display
behaviors: avoiding situations where I might fail

patient.query: I mean, I know that my friends don’t really care about grades like that, but it’s hard for me to believe it when I'm in the moment. It’s like, I can rationalize it, but deep down, I still feel that pressure to be perfect. I guess I worry more about what I think they see, rather than what they actually think. It makes it tough to separate reality from what’s in my head.

automatic_thoughts: my friends don’t really care about grades
I still feel that pressure to be perfect
I worry more about what I think they see

patient.query: Yeah, I guess it’s just this lingering fear of not living up to expectations. I mean, I put so much pressure on myself to succeed, and when I don’t, it feels like I’ve let everyone down, even if they’re not actually upset with me. Sometimes I think about how disappointed I’d be in myself if I didn’t achieve what I set out to do, and that just adds to the weight of it all. It’s like a cycle I can’t break.

automatic_thoughts: I have to live up to expectations
I put pressure on myself to succeed
I've let everyone down
They might be upset with me
I'd be disappointed in myself if I don't achieve my goals

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
